title: elymus stebbinsii subsp. stebbinsii
intro: Elymus stebbinsii subsp. stebbinsii, also known as Stebbins' wildrye, is a perennial grass native to California in the United States. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating colds, and soothing digestive issues. Herbal preparations of Elymus stebbinsii subsp. stebbinsii typically involve making teas, infusions, or tinctures from the plant's leaves and stems, although its use as a medicinal plant requires further scientific study and verification.
